_comment: Provenance manifest for trial-eval. Each source is publicly accessible at the URL given. Nothing copyrighted is redistributed in this repo — fetch_materials.py downloads to materials/cache/ at run time and run_eval.py reads from there. The cache is git-ignored.

case.name: Musk v. Altman et al.
case.court: United States District Court for the Northern District of California

sources.title: OpenAI: 'OpenAI and Elon Musk' (March 2024)
sources.description: OpenAI's public response to Musk's lawsuit, including selected 2015–2018 email correspondence between Musk, Altman, Brockman, and Sutskever.
sources.license: Publicly published by defendant; reproduces emails defendant has elected to disclose.

sources.title: OpenAI: 'Elon Musk wanted an OpenAI for-profit' (December 2024)
sources.description: Additional 2015–2017 email correspondence published by OpenAI, focused on Musk's own statements about for-profit structure and funding.

sources.title: Verified Complaint (initial)
sources.description: Musk's original complaint as filed in San Francisco Superior Court (later refiled in federal court).

sources.title: Statement of Interest of the United States (FTC)
sources.description: The FTC's filed statement of interest in the case, addressing competition concerns.

sources.title: Wikipedia: Musk v. Altman
sources.description: Tertiary summary of facts and procedural history. Useful as a neutral overview, but secondary; primary sources above are authoritative.

sources.title: OpenAI Charter
sources.description: OpenAI's published charter, central to the breach-of-mission claim.

_phase_2_additions_placeholder: After Altman's testimony, add: Altman trial transcript exhibits (PACER), Brockman testimony, Birchall testimony. Update this manifest and re-fetch.
_phase_3_additions_placeholder: After verdict, add: jury verdict form (PACER), final judgment, any post-trial findings of fact.
